summary: First-finish smoke test for Aria Vesper portfolio (static content + /api/contact). All 5 backend endpoints and 14/15 frontend flows verified. Contact form end-to-end (UI -> API -> Mongo persistence) works. One low-priority UX gap: empty-form submit relies on native HTML5 required validation instead of a visible error toast.
passed: GET /api/contact -> 200 list, submitted T1 message persisted in Mongo
Frontend loads, preloader animates and disappears
Hero CTAs 'hero-cta-view-work' and 'hero-cta-download-resume' visible; clicking view-work scrolls page (scrollY 4320)
Nav link 'nav-link-contact' scrolls to contact section (scrollY 8742)
Skills tool chips present: Figma, Adobe XD, Framer, Miro, Canva
Contact form submit with valid data shows success toast 'Message sent. I'll reply within 48 hours.' and record is stored in MongoDB (verified via GET /api/contact)
Footer text 'Designed & Developed with passion' rendered

failed.issue: Empty-form submit does not display an in-app error toast; submission is blocked by browser native HTML5 'required' validation instead. Functionally does NOT submit (correct), but no toast matching 'error/required/please' was found in body text.
failed.evidence: EMPTY_SUBMIT_ERROR_INDICATION: False after clicking contact-submit with all fields empty; no /api/contact POST fired.

failed.issue: Exact-text match for hero heading 'Designing intuitive digital experiences that people love.' returned not-visible via Playwright text= locator, likely because the kinetic masked line reveal splits characters/words into separate spans. Visually the heading renders correctly (confirmed in screenshot flow).
failed.evidence: HERO_HEADING_VISIBLE: False from text= locator; hero section otherwise interactive (CTAs visible, view-work scroll works).

action_items: Optional: emit an in-app toast (e.g., 'Please fill all required fields') when contact-submit is clicked with empty required inputs, instead of relying solely on native HTML5 validation, to match the spec's 'shows error toast' requirement.
Optional: add data-testid='hero-heading' on the heading container so automation can assert visibility without depending on the split-span text nodes.
